title: Close the loop seams advisor #54 found: forward next_action, no phantom terminal gate, preamble parity, per-session dedup, README safety truth

truths: start-feature hands off forward, never orders another hive route
a terminal record emits no re-route and no phantom gate in BOTH reminder and preamble
reminder dedup is keyed per session
README states the real bypass level matrix
prohibitions: never weaken a real gate — only phantom signals are removed

trace.outcome: An external Codex advisor disputed my 'closed completely' claim and was right on every point — I verified each mechanically rather than defending. Two of my three 1.11.1 fixes were half-done: start-feature moved the entry correctly but still wrote next_action='Invoke bee-hive', so the loop simply moved one step later; and the review-gate fix touched only the reminder, missing the preamble read at startup and after every compaction. Fixed here: next_action hands off forward, a terminal record emits no re-route and no phantom gate in BOTH surfaces, the reminder dedup is keyed per session so two sessions stop invalidating each other, and the README's 'absolute and not configurable' safety promise is replaced by the real level matrix — a documentation lie about safety. Three further advisor findings (dispatch schema, single hook source, lane-aware writes) rest on Codex-runtime facts I cannot verify from here and are surfaced with evidence rather than guessed.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "red_failure_evidence": "An external Codex advisor (issue #54) disputed my claim that the three P0 loops were 'closed completely'. I verified every claim mechanically rather than defending, and the advisor was RIGHT on all of them — two of my three 1.11.1 fixes were only half-done. Reproduced before this cell: (a) startFeature wrote next_action = 'Invoke bee-hive for \"demo\" (phase: exploring).' (state.mjs:1823), so the atomic entry I added then ordered the agent straight back into routing on the user's NEXT turn — the idle->hive loop simply moved one step later; (b) a completely fresh idle repo still emitted 'next: Invoke bee-hive.' AND 'gate pending: context' — a phantom gate for a feature that does not exist; (c) buildSessionPreamble still listed 'review: pending' because my 1.11.1 fix touched only buildPromptReminder, missing the surface read at startup and after every compaction; (d) the reminder dedup key was the repo-global string 'prompt', so two sessions on one checkout each invalidated the other's hash, collapsing the 30-minute throttle to nearly every turn; (e) README:190 promised the safety floor is 'absolute and not configurable' while bypass levels full/total exist precisely to remove it.",
  "what_i_got_wrong": "My 1.11.1 claim was overstated. P0#2 (sessionId threading) was genuinely complete — the advisor agreed. P0#5 (review gate) was fixed in ONE of two surfaces. P0#1 (idle->exploring) moved the entry correctly but left the loop-back message intact, so the loop persisted. Recording this plainly: the fix was partial and the review caught it.",
  "green_evidence": "Reproduced after the fix in a temp repo: fresh idle now reads 'next: No active bee work — awaiting a user request.' with NO gate line; the idle preamble reads 'Gates: none pending (no active work)'; and after start-feature the next turn reads 'next: Continue bee-exploring for \"demo\".' with 'gate pending: context' — the one gate that is genuinely owed. Full chain 83 suites green both with and without the agent-name prefix.",
  "tests": "Two new regression tests, both passing. test_misc.mjs asserts a terminal record emits no re-route order and no phantom gate in BOTH surfaces (reminder and preamble), while an active pre-execution phase still reports its real open gate. test_cli_state.mjs asserts start-feature's next_action never orders another hive route and hands off forward naming the feature.",
  "readme_correction": "The safety-floor section is rewritten as a level matrix (off/normal/full/total) stating exactly what each level stops for, and says outright that the earlier 'absolute and not configurable' wording was wrong because full/total exist to remove the floor. A second copy of the same false claim later in the file was corrected too. This is a documentation lie about SAFETY — the most important kind to fix.",
  "deferred_with_reason": "Three advisor findings are NOT fixed here, deliberately, because each rests on a claim about the Codex runtime that I cannot verify from this environment and a blind change could break dispatch or disable hooks outright: (1) the dispatch payload schema — bee's own doc (swarming-reference.md:134) says {task_name, message, fork_turns} while dispatch-prepare emits {agent_type, message} with no task_name, and the guard's own comment says it evaluates 'agent_type + message only — by design'; the internal contradiction is real and verified, but which side is correct depends on Codex's CURRENT schema. (2) single hook source — hooks/hooks.json exists at the plugin default location AND onboarding merges .codex/hooks.json on a comment that Codex lacks plugin hooks; whether Codex loads both is a runtime fact I cannot confirm. (3) lane-aware mutations — sessions read their lane correctly since 1.11.1 but writes still target the default record. All three are surfaced to the owner with the evidence rather than guessed at."
}
